items.prompt:
A student sends a file across a school network.

Path options
```
Chromebook -> Wi-Fi router -> school server -> teacher laptop
```
Show how the learner would describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario.
items.expected_evidence: describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A classroom app stops working because a login or system layer matters.

System note
```
App says: 'Cannot load assignment data'
Status page: network online, login token expired
```
Explain the setup and show how the learner would connect a defensive mechanism to the specific threat it is intended to reduce.
items.expected_evidence: connect a defensive mechanism to the specific threat it is intended to reduce
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates connect a defensive mechanism to the specific threat it is intended to reduce with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A security scenario involves access control and a possible attack.

Security brief
```
Shared club account password was posted in a group chat.
One student changed the page settings.
```
Show how the learner would recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs.
items.expected_evidence: recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt: Why is a list of security tools not enough to explain cybersecurity?
items.expected_evidence: because cybersecurity reasoning must connect a protective mechanism to a specific bounded threat or vulnerability, not just name tools.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to because cybersecurity reasoning must connect a protective mechanism to a specific bounded threat or vulnerability, not just name tools.

items.prompt: What does a threat model help you decide?
items.expected_evidence: it helps identify what the system is being protected from so the chosen defenses match the actual risk rather than a generic fear.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to it helps identify what the system is being protected from so the chosen defenses match the actual risk rather than a generic fear.

items.prompt:
In a network or security scenario, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner names security tools or practices without connecting them to a bounded threat model.

Security artifact
```
Asset: shared class account
Event: password posted publicly
Result: settings changed by another user
```
Diagnose the systems or cybersecurity mistake. Focus on tool-list security.
items.expected_evidence: describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario.

items.prompt:
In a network or security scenario, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner cannot explain why a particular defensive mechanism fits the stated risk.

Security artifact
```
Asset: shared class account
Event: password posted publicly
Result: settings changed by another user
```
Diagnose the systems or cybersecurity mistake. Focus on threat-mechanism mismatch.
items.expected_evidence: connect a defensive mechanism to the specific threat it is intended to reduce
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward connect a defensive mechanism to the specific threat it is intended to reduce.

items.prompt:
A network or security idea learned with classroom file sharing is now embedded in cloud storage.

Cloud case
```
Phone uploads photo -> cloud storage -> shared album -> friend views image
```
Explain how the learner would recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs.
items.expected_evidence: recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves recognize that security controls can involve usability, cost, or performance tradeoffs in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
A learner must move from a visible device-level description to a more abstract threat-model case.

Threat-model prompt
```
Asset: grades database
Threat: stolen staff password
Defense options: MFA, stronger password, unplug Wi-Fi
```
Show how they would avoid naming tools without explaining what attack surface or risk they address.
items.expected_evidence: avoid naming tools without explaining what attack surface or risk they address
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves avoid naming tools without explaining what attack surface or risk they address in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
After a delay, the learner is given a fresh connected-system or security case and must explain the pathway, layer, risk, or defense involved.

Delayed case
```
Student tablet -> hotspot -> video app -> streaming server
```
Show how they would describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario.
items.expected_evidence: describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates describe who or what the system is being protected from in a bounded scenario on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt:
Use this short systems or access-control artifact on systems architecture, resource constraints, and platform layers before closing cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning.

Probe artifact
```
Login page -> app dashboard -> cloud document -> share settings
```
Decide whether the current problem is actually an upstream dependency gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether systems architecture, resource constraints, and platform layers is secure enough to support cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in systems architecture, resource constraints, and platform layers from a current-node issue in cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: systems architecture, resource constraints, and platform layers

items.prompt:
Use this short systems or access-control artifact on specification, testing, and program quality criteria before closing cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning.

Probe artifact
```
Login page -> app dashboard -> cloud document -> share settings
```
Decide whether the current problem is actually an upstream dependency gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether specification, testing, and program quality criteria is secure enough to support cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in specification, testing, and program quality criteria from a current-node issue in cybersecurity mechanisms, threat models, and defensive reasoning.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: specification, testing, and program quality criteria
